stemHtml: Choose the most correct statement regarding polar stereographic charts…

options.a: A great circle line is between the straight line and the rhumb line, and always concave to the pole.
options.b: A great circle line is always below the straight line, and always concave to the equator.
options.c: Straight line is represented between the great circle line and the rhumb line.
options.d: From north to south, the great circle is always a straight line like the rhumb line.
